kings_guild.name: The King's Guild
kings_guild.description: Master artisans of the King's Guild each ply a different trade in a different quarter of the city. Work out who's who.
clues: Potter goes with at least one of Kingsford or 13 coins.
Aldric's levy is exactly 4 coins more than the Weaver.
Either Aldric goes with Riverside or someone in the Ironmongers' Guild goes with Rowan (but not both).
Either Southgate goes with Potter or someone in the North Side goes with Cedric (but not both).
Exactly one of these is true: Cedric goes with 10 coins; Blacksmith goes with Kingsford; Tanner goes with Millpond.
Highrow goes with exactly two of Aldric, Fletcher, and 14 coins.
More members of the Ironmongers' Guild are in the River Ward than members of the Joiners' Guild.
11 coins goes with exactly two of Beatrix, Potter, and Southgate.
If Blacksmith does not go with 13 coins, then the artisan with 15 coins belongs to the River Ward.
Wulfric goes with exactly one of Blacksmith, Tanner, or 12 coins.
Bridgegate, Millpond, and 10 coins go with Blacksmith, Tanner, and Weaver, in some order.
Bridgegate goes with either Matilda or Rowan.
11 coins, 13 coins, and 14 coins go with Aldric, Rowan, and Southgate, in some order.

fishing.name: The Fishing Derby
fishing.description: Anglers at the lake-country derby each landed a different fish from a different pond on a different lure. Work out who caught what, and where.
clues: Crappie goes with at least one of Grayreach or 13 lb.
Boone's weight is exactly 4 lb more than the angler who landed the Pike.
Either Boone goes with Pinehollow or someone in the Pike Family goes with Royce (but not both).
Either Stillwater goes with Crappie or someone in the North Watershed goes with Gus (but not both).
Exactly one of these is true: Gus goes with 10 lb; Bass goes with Grayreach; Perch goes with Mossbank.
Foxglen goes with exactly two of Boone, Bluegill, and 14 lb.
More members of the Pike Family are in the East Watershed than members of the Sunfish Family.
11 lb goes with exactly two of Esther, Crappie, and Stillwater.
